description: An artifact (any class) using a telemetry token (CALIBRATION_IMPROVED or CALIBRATION_DEGRADED) as its per-claim status MUST receive INVALID. Telemetry statuses are system-level signals, not artifact statuses (§9).

input.claim: this artifact matches its declared origin digest

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 §9 Status Registry: 'Telemetry statuses such as CALIBRATION_IMPROVED and CALIBRATION_DEGRADED are system telemetry, not per-claim statuses.' The artifact above sets status='CALIBRATION_IMPROVED', which is not a member of the Class A status registry (VERIFIED, TAMPERED, INVALID) — nor of any per-class status registry. A conformant verifier MUST reject the artifact as INVALID. The same rule applies to any class: telemetry tokens MUST NOT be emitted as artifact status values. Class A is selected for concreteness in this vector; the underlying invariant is class-agnostic, hence the 'class-any' prefix on the vector_id.
